ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role (e.g., admin, moderator, user) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date the user registered */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "profile_image" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile image */,
  "bio" TEXT /* User's bio or description */,
  "social_links" TEXT /* Social media links of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the user (e.g., active, suspended, banned) */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Date of the user's last activity */,
  "reputation_score" INTEGER /* Reputation score of the user based on community interactions */,
  "notification_preferences" TEXT /* User preferences for notifications (e.g., email, push notifications) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, moderator, user) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its permissions */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* List of permissions associated with the role (e.g., can_moderate, can_edit_posts) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forums" (
  "forum_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forum */,
  "forum_name" TEXT /* Name of the forum (e.g., UB-League, General Discussion, User Blogs) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the forum */,
  "moderator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is the moderator of the forum */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the forum was created */,
  "parent_forum_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent forum (if any) */,
  "is_locked" INTEGER /* Indicates if the forum is locked for new posts */,
  "view_count" INTEGER /* Number of views the forum has received */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forum_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forums_parent_forum_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_forum_id") REFERENCES forums ("forum_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forums_moderator_id FOREIGN KEY ("moderator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "posts" (
  "post_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each post */,
  "forum_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forum where the post was made */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the post */,
  "topic" TEXT /* Topic or title of the post */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the post */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the post has received */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views the post has received */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the post was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the post was last updated */,
  "is_sticky" INTEGER /* Indicates if the post is sticky (pinned to the top) */,
  "is_locked" INTEGER /* Indicates if the post is locked for new replies */,
  "reported_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the post has been reported */,
  "is_hidden" INTEGER /* Indicates if the post is hidden from public view */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_forum_id FOREIGN KEY ("forum_id") REFERENCES forums ("forum_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_posts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "replies" (
  "reply_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each reply */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post being replied to */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the reply */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the reply */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the reply has received */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the reply was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the reply was last updated */,
  "reported_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the reply has been reported */,
  "is_hidden" INTEGER /* Indicates if the reply is hidden from public view */,
  "is_edited" INTEGER /* Indicates if the reply has been edited */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reply_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_replies_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "likes" (
  "like_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each like */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who liked the content */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post being liked (NULL if it's a reply) */,
  "reply_id" INTEGER /* ID of the reply being liked (NULL if it's a post) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the like was created */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Indicates if the like has been deleted (e.g., user unliked) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("like_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_likes_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_likes_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_likes_reply_id FOREIGN KEY ("reply_id") REFERENCES replies ("reply_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "views" (
  "view_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each view */,
  "post_id" INTEGER /* ID of the post being viewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who viewed the post */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the view was recorded */,
  "is_deleted" INTEGER /* Indicates if the view has been deleted (e.g., user deleted their account) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("view_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_views_post_id FOREIGN KEY ("post_id") REFERENCES posts ("post_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_views_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the event or tournament */,
  "forum_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forum where the event is announced */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the event */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the event */,
  "organizer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who organized the event */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the event was created */,
  "is_cancelled" INTEGER /* Indicates if the event has been cancelled */,
  "registration_start_date" TEXT /* Start date for event registration */,
  "registration_end_date" TEXT /* End date for event registration */,
  "max_participants" INTEGER /* Maximum number of participants allowed */,
  "current_participants" INTEGER /* Current number of participants */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_organizer_id FOREIGN KEY ("organizer_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_forum_id FOREIGN KEY ("forum_id") REFERENCES forums ("forum_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "event_participants" (
  "participant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each participant */,
  "event_id" INTEGER /* ID of the event the user is participating in */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user participating in the event */,
  "registered_date" TEXT /* Date the user registered for the event */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the participant (e.g., registered, confirmed, withdrawn) */,
  "is_confirmed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the participant has confirmed their participation */,
  "is_checked_in" INTEGER /* Indicates if the participant has checked in on the event day */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("participant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_participants_event_id FOREIGN KEY ("event_id") REFERENCES events ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_event_participants_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_blogs" (
  "blog_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each blog post */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the blog */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the blog post */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the blog post */,
  "likes" INTEGER /* Number of likes the blog post has received */,
  "views" INTEGER /* Number of views the blog post has received */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the blog post was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the blog post was last updated */,
  "is_private" INTEGER /* Indicates if the blog post is private (visible only to the user) */,
  "is_hidden" INTEGER /* Indicates if the blog post is hidden from public view */,
  "reported_count" INTEGER /* Number of times the blog post has been reported */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("blog_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_blogs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
